item.title: Welcome to the ‘E-shaped’ economy: Wealth gap is no longer between just high and low earners, the middle class is also struggling out on its own
item.description: A look at BofA's data shows the shape of the consumer economy is no longer a K—if we're sticking with the alphabet theme, one might suggest an 'E' is ...

item.title: Some folks on Wall Street think yesterday’s U.S. jobs number was ‘implausible’ and thus due for a downward correction
item.description: Some analysts are worrying that the latest number might be wrong, and that the level of job creation in America is lower than the stats suggest.

item.title: The CEO of Capgemini has a warning. You might be thinking about AI all wrong
item.description: Aiman Ezzat warns that AI FOMO could cost companies dearly if they treat it as a hype instead of a true business transformation.

item.title: How Trump erased the story of George Washington’s slave, Ona Judge, who fled from Philadelphia to freedom
item.description: In late January, the National Park Service dismantled the slavery exhibit at Philadelphia Independence National Historic Park.

item.title: 3 factors that will separate the ‘SaaSpocalypse’ winners from losers
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.
